Package org.apache.juneau.marshall.json

Class JsonTokenReader

java.lang.Object
org.apache.juneau.marshall.json.JsonTokenReader
All Implemented Interfaces:
Closeable, AutoCloseable, RecordReader, TokenReader
Direct Known Subclasses:
Json5TokenReader, JsonlTokenReader
public class JsonTokenReader extends Object implements TokenReader
Reference implementation of the public TokenReader surface for the JSON format.

Backed by the existing internal ParserReader plumbing. Operates as a hand-rolled state machine over the JSON grammar — structural tokens ({, }, [, ], ,, :) and the four scalar shapes (string, number, boolean, null).

Pipe-level settings (charset, auto-close, unbuffered) are configured on the ParserPipe the caller hands in. Cursor-level behavior is driven by a small JsonTokenReader.Settings bundle — currently just trimStrings.

Notes:
  • This class is not thread safe.
  • Whitespace and JSON comments are tolerated — comments are silently skipped to mirror Juneau's existing JSON parser behavior.
  • The cursor is purely structural; object swaps and @Schema annotations have no effect. Use the POJO databind path on JsonParser for those.

    Description
    static final record 
    JsonTokenReader.Settings
    Cursor-level settings honored by JsonTokenReader.

    Modifier and Type
    Field
    Description
    protected boolean
    currentBoolean
    Boolean value buffer for the current TokenType.VALUE_BOOLEAN token.
    protected String
    currentFieldName
    Field name buffer for the current TokenType.FIELD_NAME token.
    protected String
    currentNumberLexeme
    Numeric lexeme buffer for the current TokenType.VALUE_NUMBER token (preserved verbatim from the input).
    protected String
    currentString
    String value buffer for the current TokenType.VALUE_STRING token.
    protected TokenType
    currentToken
    The token most recently emitted by next().
    protected int
    depth
    Current container nesting depth (0 = root).
    protected static final String
    MSG_invalidNumber
    Error-message format string for invalid JSON numbers.
    protected static final String
    MSG_unexpectedChar
    Error-message format string for unexpected characters at the cursor's current position.
    protected final ParserReader
    r
    Underlying character-level reader; subclasses use this for direct read/unread/mark/getMarked calls during dialect-specific tokenization.
    protected static final int
    S00_expectValue
    State-machine state: at root, after [, after :, or after , inside an array.
    protected static final int
    S01_expectFieldName
    State-machine state: after { or after , inside an object.
    protected static final int
    S02_expectColon
    State-machine state: just emitted a TokenType.FIELD_NAME.
    protected static final int
    S03_expectCommaOrEndObject
    State-machine state: just emitted a value inside an object.
    protected static final int
    S04_expectCommaOrEndArray
    State-machine state: just emitted a value inside an array.
    protected static final int
    S05_end
    State-machine state: the root document has been fully consumed.
    protected final JsonTokenReader.Settings
    settings
    Cursor-level settings (currently just trimStrings).
    protected int
    state
    Current state-machine state; one of the STATE_* constants.

    Description

    Builds a ParseException with the given message + MessageFormat-style arguments.

    popContainer()
    Pops the top container off the cursor's depth stack.

    pushContainer(boolean isArray)
    Pushes a new container onto the cursor's depth stack.
    <T> T
    read(Class<T> type)
    Consumes the upcoming value (object, array, or scalar) and binds it to a POJO via the format's existing parser.
    <T> T
    read(Type type, Type... args)
    Same as TokenReader.read(Class) but accepts a parameterized Type and optional type arguments (the same shape as Parser.parse(Object, Type, Type...)).
    <T> T
    read(ClassMeta<T> type)
    Same as TokenReader.read(Class) but accepts a pre-resolved ClassMeta.

    readCommaOrEndArray(int c)
    State-machine dispatch: handles the next character at S04_expectCommaOrEndArray.

    State-machine dispatch: handles the next character at S03_expectCommaOrEndObject.

    State-machine dispatch: handles the next character at S01_expectFieldName.

  • Field Details

    • MSG_invalidNumber

      protected static final String MSG_invalidNumber
      Error-message format string for invalid JSON numbers. Used with parseException(String, Object...).
      See Also:
      • Constant Field Values

    • MSG_unexpectedChar

      protected static final String MSG_unexpectedChar
      Error-message format string for unexpected characters at the cursor's current position. Used with parseException(String, Object...).
      See Also:
      • Constant Field Values

    • S00_expectValue

      protected static final int S00_expectValue
      State-machine state: at root, after [, after :, or after , inside an array. The next read should be a JSON value (object, array, or scalar).
      See Also:
      • Constant Field Values

    • S01_expectFieldName

      protected static final int S01_expectFieldName
      State-machine state: after { or after , inside an object. The next read should be a field name (string).
      See Also:
      • Constant Field Values

    • S02_expectColon

      protected static final int S02_expectColon
      State-machine state: just emitted a TokenType.FIELD_NAME. The next char should be :.
      See Also:
      • Constant Field Values

    • S03_expectCommaOrEndObject

      protected static final int S03_expectCommaOrEndObject
      State-machine state: just emitted a value inside an object. The next char should be , (followed by another field name) or }.
      See Also:
      • Constant Field Values

    • S04_expectCommaOrEndArray

      protected static final int S04_expectCommaOrEndArray
      State-machine state: just emitted a value inside an array. The next char should be , (followed by another value) or ].
      See Also:
      • Constant Field Values

    • S05_end

      protected static final int S05_end
      State-machine state: the root document has been fully consumed. The next call to next() returns TokenType.END_OF_STREAM.
      See Also:
      • Constant Field Values

    • r

      protected final ParserReader r
      Underlying character-level reader; subclasses use this for direct read/unread/mark/getMarked calls during dialect-specific tokenization.

    • settings

      protected final JsonTokenReader.Settings settings
      Cursor-level settings (currently just trimStrings).

    • currentToken

      protected TokenType currentToken
      The token most recently emitted by next(). Subclasses set this when overriding state-machine dispatch methods.

    • state

      protected int state
      Current state-machine state; one of the STATE_* constants.

    • depth

      protected int depth
      Current container nesting depth (0 = root).

    • currentFieldName

      protected String currentFieldName
      Field name buffer for the current TokenType.FIELD_NAME token.

    • currentString

      protected String currentString
      String value buffer for the current TokenType.VALUE_STRING token.

    • currentNumberLexeme

      protected String currentNumberLexeme
      Numeric lexeme buffer for the current TokenType.VALUE_NUMBER token (preserved verbatim from the input).

    • currentBoolean

      protected boolean currentBoolean
      Boolean value buffer for the current TokenType.VALUE_BOOLEAN token.

  • Constructor Details

    • JsonTokenReader

      public JsonTokenReader(ParserPipe pipe) throws IOException
      Constructor with default JsonTokenReader.Settings.
      Parameters:
      pipe - The parser input pipe to read from. Must not be null.
      Throws:
      IOException - If a problem occurred opening the underlying reader.

    • JsonTokenReader

      public JsonTokenReader(ParserPipe pipe, JsonTokenReader.Settings settings) throws IOException
      Constructor with explicit JsonTokenReader.Settings.
      Parameters:
      pipe - The parser input pipe to read from. Must not be null.
      settings - The cursor-level settings. Must not be null.
      Throws:
      IOException - If a problem occurred opening the underlying reader.

    • JsonTokenReader

      public JsonTokenReader(ParserPipe pipe, JsonTokenReader.Settings settings, JsonParserSession session) throws IOException
      Constructor used by JsonParser.parseTokens(Object) to plumb the calling parser's session in so that read(Class) can delegate to the existing databind path.
      Parameters:
      pipe - The parser input pipe to read from. Must not be null.
      settings - The cursor-level settings. Must not be null.
      session - The JsonParserSession to use for read(Class) delegation, or null to disable read on this reader (it will throw UnsupportedOperationException).
      Throws:
      IOException - If a problem occurred opening the underlying reader.

  • Method Details

    • next

      public TokenType next() throws IOException, ParseException
      Description copied from interface: TokenReader
      Advances the cursor to the next token.
      Specified by:
      next in interface TokenReader
      Returns:
      The token type of the new current token, or TokenType.END_OF_STREAM when the underlying input is exhausted. Never returns TokenType.NOT_AVAILABLE — that value is reserved for the initial pre-next() state.
      Throws:
      IOException - If a problem occurred reading from the underlying stream.
      ParseException - If the input is malformed for the format.

    • getCurrentToken

      public TokenType getCurrentToken()
      Description copied from interface: TokenReader
      Returns the current token type without advancing.
      Specified by:
      getCurrentToken in interface TokenReader
      Returns:
      The current token type. Returns TokenType.NOT_AVAILABLE before the first call to TokenReader.next(), and TokenType.END_OF_STREAM once the stream is exhausted.

    • getDepth

      public int getDepth()
      Description copied from interface: TokenReader
      Returns the current depth of the cursor.

      Depth starts at 0 at the document root and increases by 1 for each TokenType.START_OBJECT / TokenType.START_ARRAY encountered (and decreases on the matching end tokens).

      Specified by:
      getDepth in interface TokenReader
      Returns:
      The current depth.

    • getFieldName

      public String getFieldName()
      Description copied from interface: TokenReader
      Returns the field name when the current token is TokenType.FIELD_NAME.
      Specified by:
      getFieldName in interface TokenReader
      Returns:
      The field name.

    • getString

      public String getString()
      Description copied from interface: TokenReader
      Returns the current scalar value as a string.

      Defined for TokenType.VALUE_STRING (the natural value), TokenType.FIELD_NAME (returns the field name), TokenType.VALUE_NUMBER (returns the lexeme), and TokenType.VALUE_BOOLEAN (returns "true"/"false"). For TokenType.VALUE_NULL returns null.

      Specified by:
      getString in interface TokenReader
      Returns:
      The string view of the current value.

    • getNumber

      public Number getNumber() throws ParseException
      Description copied from interface: TokenReader
      Returns the current numeric value as a parsed Number.

      Implementations may parse lazily; the returned Number subtype is implementation- and format-defined (e.g. Long / Double / BigDecimal). Use TokenReader.getNumberLexeme() to recover the original textual form.

      Specified by:
      getNumber in interface TokenReader
      Returns:
      The parsed numeric value.
      Throws:
      ParseException - If the lexeme cannot be parsed.

    • getNumberLexeme

      public String getNumberLexeme()
      Description copied from interface: TokenReader
      Returns the original numeric lexeme for the current numeric token.

      For text formats this is the exact substring that produced the number. For binary formats the implementation returns a canonical text rendering of the native numeric token.

      Specified by:
      getNumberLexeme in interface TokenReader
      Returns:
      The numeric lexeme.

    • getBool

      public boolean getBool()
      Description copied from interface: TokenReader
      Returns the current boolean value.
      Specified by:
      getBool in interface TokenReader
      Returns:
      The boolean value.

    • getBinary

      public byte[] getBinary()
      Description copied from interface: TokenReader
      Returns the current binary value.

      Binary scalars come from binary formats only (cbor, msgpack, bson, ...). Text formats deliver embedded binary content as base64-encoded TokenType.VALUE_STRING.

      Specified by:
      getBinary in interface TokenReader
      Returns:
      The binary value.

    • canRead

      public boolean canRead() throws IOException, ParseException
      Description copied from interface: TokenReader
      Returns true if TokenReader.read(Class) is currently callable on this cursor — i.e. the cursor is positioned at a value boundary.

      This is the natural loop predicate when streaming records:

      try (TokenReader r = Json.DEFAULT.parseTokens(input)) { r.next(); // consume the opening [ while (r.canRead()) { Item item = r.read(Item.class); process(item); } r.next(); // consume the closing ] }

      Specified by:
      canRead in interface RecordReader
      Specified by:
      canRead in interface TokenReader
      Returns:
      true if the next operation can be read; false if the cursor is positioned at a structural separator, field name, end-container, or end-of-stream.
      Throws:
      IOException - If a problem occurred peeking at the underlying stream.
      ParseException - If the input is malformed for the format.

    • allowsTrailingComma

      protected boolean allowsTrailingComma()
      Subclass extension point: returns whether trailing commas are accepted before ] / }.

      Default returns false (strict RFC-8259 JSON). Json5TokenReader overrides this to return true for the JSON5 dialect.

      Returns:
      true if a trailing comma should be silently accepted; false if it should produce a parse error.

    • read

      public <T> T read(Class<T> type) throws IOException, ParseException
      Description copied from interface: TokenReader
      Consumes the upcoming value (object, array, or scalar) and binds it to a POJO via the format's existing parser.

      Cursor positioning: the cursor must be in a state where the next call to TokenReader.next() would emit a value-shaped token (i.e. TokenType.START_OBJECT, TokenType.START_ARRAY, or one of the VALUE_* tokens). Calling read consumes that upcoming value wholesale and advances the cursor's depth back to where it was before the value would have started — structurally equivalent to calling TokenReader.next() on a scalar, or TokenReader.next() + TokenReader.skipChildren() on a container.

      Do NOT call TokenReader.next() before read. Once the structural opener (e.g. { or [) has been consumed by TokenReader.next(), the underlying parser cannot re-bind the value. The contract is "instead-of-next, not after-next."

      Default behavior: the default implementation throws UnsupportedOperationException. Format-specific token readers override this by delegating to their existing *ParserSession at the cursor's current input position and resyncing the cursor's state machine afterward.

      Specified by:
      read in interface RecordReader
      Specified by:
      read in interface TokenReader
      Type Parameters:
      T - The expected return type.
      Parameters:
      type - The target Java type.
      Returns:
      The bound POJO.
      Throws:
      IOException - If a problem occurred reading from the underlying stream.
      ParseException - If the input is malformed for the format or cannot be bound to the type.

    • read

      public <T> T read(ClassMeta<T> type) throws IOException, ParseException
      Description copied from interface: TokenReader
      Same as TokenReader.read(Class) but accepts a pre-resolved ClassMeta.
      Specified by:
      read in interface RecordReader
      Specified by:
      read in interface TokenReader
      Type Parameters:
      T - The expected return type.
      Parameters:
      type - The target type.
      Returns:
      The bound POJO.
      Throws:
      IOException - If a problem occurred reading from the underlying stream.
      ParseException - If the input is malformed or cannot be bound to the type.

    • read

      public <T> T read(Type type, Type... args) throws IOException, ParseException
      Description copied from interface: TokenReader
      Same as TokenReader.read(Class) but accepts a parameterized Type and optional type arguments (the same shape as Parser.parse(Object, Type, Type...)).
      Specified by:
      read in interface RecordReader
      Specified by:
      read in interface TokenReader
      Type Parameters:
      T - The expected return type.
      Parameters:
      type - The target type.
      args - Optional type arguments for parameterized types.
      Returns:
      The bound POJO.
      Throws:
      IOException - If a problem occurred reading from the underlying stream.
      ParseException - If the input is malformed or cannot be bound to the type.

    • skipChildren

      public void skipChildren() throws IOException, ParseException
      Description copied from interface: TokenReader
      Skips over the children of the current structural token.

      If the current token is TokenType.START_OBJECT or TokenType.START_ARRAY, the cursor is advanced to the matching TokenType.END_OBJECT or TokenType.END_ARRAY (i.e. the entire sub-tree is consumed). For any other token this is a no-op.

      Specified by:
      skipChildren in interface TokenReader
      Throws:
      IOException - If a problem occurred reading from the underlying stream.
      ParseException - If the input is malformed for the format.

    • close

      public void close() throws IOException
      Description copied from interface: TokenReader
      Closes the underlying stream.

      Implementations should be idempotent — closing a closed reader is a no-op.

      Specified by:
      close in interface AutoCloseable
      Specified by:
      close in interface Closeable
      Specified by:
      close in interface RecordReader
      Specified by:
      close in interface TokenReader
      Throws:
      IOException

    • readValueStart

      protected void readValueStart(int c) throws IOException, ParseException
      State-machine dispatch: handles the next character at S00_expectValue.

      Subclasses (e.g. Json5TokenReader) override this to add dialect-specific value shapes (single-quoted strings, bare identifiers, missing-value handling).

      Parameters:
      c - The next non-whitespace, non-comment character read from the input.
      Throws:
      IOException - If a problem occurred reading from the underlying stream.
      ParseException - If the input is malformed at this position.

    • readFieldNameOrEndObject

      protected void readFieldNameOrEndObject(int c) throws IOException, ParseException
      State-machine dispatch: handles the next character at S01_expectFieldName.

      Subclasses (e.g. Json5TokenReader) override this to accept bare-identifier field names and single-quoted field names.

      Parameters:
      c - The next non-whitespace character read from the input.
      Throws:
      IOException - If a problem occurred reading from the underlying stream.
      ParseException - If the input is malformed at this position.

    • readCommaOrEndObject

      protected void readCommaOrEndObject(int c) throws IOException, ParseException
      State-machine dispatch: handles the next character at S03_expectCommaOrEndObject.

      Subclasses (e.g. Json5TokenReader) override this to accept trailing commas before }.

      Parameters:
      c - The next non-whitespace character read from the input.
      Throws:
      IOException - If a problem occurred reading from the underlying stream.
      ParseException - If the input is malformed at this position.

    • readCommaOrEndArray

      protected void readCommaOrEndArray(int c) throws IOException, ParseException
      State-machine dispatch: handles the next character at S04_expectCommaOrEndArray.

      Subclasses (e.g. Json5TokenReader) override this to accept trailing commas before ].

      Parameters:
      c - The next non-whitespace character read from the input.
      Throws:
      IOException - If a problem occurred reading from the underlying stream.
      ParseException - If the input is malformed at this position.

    • afterValue

      protected void afterValue()
      Sets the post-value state based on the enclosing container.

      Subclasses (e.g. JsonlTokenReader) override this when the format treats consecutive top-level values differently — JSONL transitions back to S00_expectValue at depth 0 instead of S05_end so the next line is read.

    • readString

      protected String readString() throws IOException, ParseException
      Reads a double-quoted JSON string from the current input position.

      Handles escape sequences (\\n, \\t, \\uNNNN, etc.) inline via ParserReader.replace(char). Subclasses (e.g. Json5TokenReader) override this to also accept single-quoted strings.

      Returns:
      The parsed string (without surrounding quotes).
      Throws:
      IOException - If a problem occurred reading from the underlying stream.
      ParseException - If the input is malformed at this position.

    • readKeywordValue

      protected void readKeywordValue(int c) throws IOException, ParseException
      Reads one of the JSON keyword literals (true, false, null) starting with the given lookahead character. Sets currentToken and currentBoolean accordingly.
      Parameters:
      c - The first character of the keyword (one of 't', 'f', 'n').
      Throws:
      IOException - If a problem occurred reading from the underlying stream.
      ParseException - If the keyword is malformed.

    • readNumberValue

      protected void readNumberValue() throws IOException, ParseException
      Reads a JSON number from the current input position. Captures the lexeme via ParserReader.parseNumberString() and validates it; defers actual numeric parsing to getNumber() (lazy). Sets currentNumberLexeme and currentToken.
      Throws:
      IOException - If a problem occurred reading from the underlying stream.
      ParseException - If the input is not a valid JSON number.

    • readSkipWsAndComments

      protected int readSkipWsAndComments() throws IOException, ParseException
      Reads the next non-whitespace, non-comment character or -1 at end of stream.
      Returns:
      The next significant character, or -1 at end of input.
      Throws:
      IOException - If a problem occurred reading from the underlying stream.
      ParseException - If a JSON comment is malformed.

    • isNumberStart

      protected static boolean isNumberStart(int c)
      Returns whether the given character could start a JSON number lexeme (digit or minus sign).
      Parameters:
      c - The character.
      Returns:
      true if the character could start a number.

    • isDigit

      protected static boolean isDigit(int c)
      Returns whether the given character is an ASCII decimal digit.
      Parameters:
      c - The character.
      Returns:
      true if the character is between '0' and '9' inclusive.

    • pushContainer

      protected void pushContainer(boolean isArray)
      Pushes a new container onto the cursor's depth stack.
      Parameters:
      isArray - true if the new container is an array; false for an object.

    • popContainer

      protected void popContainer() throws ParseException
      Pops the top container off the cursor's depth stack.
      Throws:
      ParseException - If the stack is already empty (defensive only; well-formed JSON cannot trigger this).

    • isInArray

      protected boolean isInArray()
      Returns whether the cursor's current container (the one most recently pushed) is an array.
      Returns:
      true if currently inside an array; false if inside an object.

    • parseException

      protected ParseException parseException(String message, Object... args)
      Builds a ParseException with the given message + MessageFormat-style arguments. Used by all dispatch methods to surface input-malformed errors with consistent formatting.
      Parameters:
      message - The exception message format string ({0}, {1}, ...).
      args - The format arguments.
      Returns:
      A new ParseException ready to throw.
